title: US plane went to Middle East after Prestwick Airport stop

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: 'Clear answers' have been demanded after a U.S military plane stopped at Prestwick Airport on the way to the Middle East amid the ongoing war with Iran . An Air Force transport plane bound for a base in southern Turkey stopped in South Ayrshire en route, with the publicly owned transport hub frequently used for military activities.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: On Saturday the United States and Israel launched a long-expected offensive against Iran, with American forces having been massing in the gulf for several weeks. The strikes have been described as illegal under international law by French President Emmanuel Macron and Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez, the latter of whom has banned American military planes from using Spanish bases.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – US military plane uses Prestwick Airport before flying to the Middle East
